description: Unique assay identifier combining GeneLab dataset accession and MD5 hash of metadata (links assay to dataset and ensures version integrity)
Human-readable assay title detailing the biological measurement performed
Platform or method used to generate assay data (e.g., sequencing platform, mass spectrometry, imaging)
Type of data produced by the assay, describing the focus of the analysis

Primary experimental factor(s) applied at the assay level (independent variables intentionally varied by the investigator); often corresponds to study-level design (e.g., Ground Control). Additional factors (e.g., dose, time point, sex, strain) may also be included.
Secondary experimental factor(s) for this assay—typically the comparative group(s) in analysis—connecting this assay to differential conditions (e.g., Space Flight).
Biological material analyzed in the first assay group (e.g., tissue, cell type, organ)
Biological material analyzed in the second assay group (e.g., tissue, cell type, organ)
Preferred ontology-based name for the first material (e.g., UBERON term)
Preferred ontology-based name for the second material (e.g., UBERON term)
Controlled ontology identifier for the first material (e.g., UBERON code)
Controlled ontology identifier for the second material (e.g., UBERON code)
